personas.name: Maya
personas.notes: Maya is talented, but her recent design work has needed too many review rounds before it feels ready.
personas.scripted_fallback: comes back to checking the full flow before sharing, not just the main screens

script.name: Before we start, is there anything specific you want to make sure we cover today?
script.answer: wants to dig into how she judges 'ready to share' — thinks that's where the rework starts

script.name: When you say 'ready to share,' what are you looking for before you bring work in?
script.answer: checks main screens. doesn't check edge cases / empty states before sharing

script.name: Can you walk me through a recent design that took more review rounds than you expected?
script.answer: onboarding dashboard. 3 review rounds. missed the edge cases in the empty states

script.name: In those empty states, what did you not see until review?
script.answer: missed the zero-data path for new users. empty state read as too generic

script.name: What part of the design usually gets discovered too late for first review?
script.answer: flow and edge cases surface late. screens look fine, review finds missing states / unclear rationale

script.name: When rationale is unclear in review, what were you still deciding as you shared it?
script.answer: still working out why one direction beats another. mostly edge cases and flow

script.name: What part of your drafting process most affects how ready the design feels on first pass?
script.answer: depends how much of the flow she checks before sharing. main screens only → gaps in review

script.name: What do you want to focus on before the next review round?
script.answer: wants to check the full flow before review. empty states, edge cases, explain the direction

personas.name: Rachel
personas.notes: Rachel is usually thoughtful, but she has been much quieter in team conversations recently.
personas.scripted_fallback: not sure it's one thing. just less lately that felt worth interrupting for

script.name: Before we start, is there anything specific you want to make sure we cover today?
script.answer: nothing specific. happy to just talk through how the last few weeks have felt

script.name: I've noticed you've been a bit quieter in team conversations lately — does that match how it feels to you?
script.answer: agrees. been holding back more than usual

script.name: When did that start to shift for you?
script.answer: shifted when research got pushed later in the cycle. findings land, decisions already feel made

script.name: When you do have something to say, what makes it feel worth raising or not?
script.answer: raises things if it'll actually change a decision. lately the timing hasn't felt there

script.name: How has the workload itself been sitting with you?
script.answer: volume's fine. more that the work feels less connected to what gets decided

script.name: What would make it feel more worth speaking up again?
script.answer: wants research in earlier, before the direction's set, so it can actually shape things

script.name: What's one thing we could try in the next couple of weeks?
script.answer: pull her into the framing stage on the next project, not just validation

personas.name: Jordan
personas.notes: Jordan wants more scope, but we need to define what stronger ownership actually means.
personas.scripted_fallback: sees ownership as driving the decision, not just running the process around it

script.name: Before we start, is there anything specific you want to make sure we cover today?
script.answer: wants to talk about taking on more scope, and what that actually needs to look like

script.name: When you say more scope, what would you actually be running that you don't now?
script.answer: running a problem area end to end, not just shipping features someone else scoped

script.name: Where have you already been driving a decision rather than running the process?
script.answer: billing flow. set the direction, brought the team along, didn't wait for sign-off each step

script.name: Where do stakeholders already come to you directly, and where do they still go around you?
script.answer: eng comes to her direct now. roadmap calls still go to her lead first

script.name: What's the gap between where you are and owning that area fully?
script.answer: gap = trusted to make the trade-off calls without checking first. clearer when she disagrees

script.name: What's a recent call you'd have made differently if it were fully yours?
script.answer: would've cut the second integration sooner. kept it alive to avoid stakeholder pushback

script.name: What's one thing you could take on next quarter to start heading that way?
script.answer: wants to own the trade-off decisions on one area outright. bring the reasoning, not the question

personas.name: Liam
personas.notes: Liam works hard, but stakeholders still seem unclear on decisions and next steps.
personas.scripted_fallback: decisions clear in his head, just not closing the loop with everyone in writing

script.name: Before we start, is there anything specific you want to make sure we cover today?
script.answer: happy to talk the stakeholder side. knows there's been confusion on where things land

script.name: After a decision, stakeholders still seem unclear on what was decided. Does that match what you see?
script.answer: agrees. happens more than he'd like. people leave the room thinking different things

script.name: Walk me through a recent decision where the alignment didn't hold.
script.answer: pricing change. thought it was agreed, but sales and finance each had a different version a week later

script.name: Between the decision and people acting on it, where does the clarity get lost?
script.answer: decides in the meeting, doesn't always send the written summary → everyone fills the gaps themselves

script.name: Whose job is it to make sure that decision is understood afterward?
script.answer: owns it. treating the meeting as the end point when it's really the start of getting everyone aligned

script.name: When there's a trade-off, how clearly are you naming what you're giving up?
script.answer: not clearly enough. presents the choice as obvious, doesn't show the cost of the other option

script.name: What's one change you'd make on the next big decision?
script.answer: will send a short written decision + owners + next steps within a day, confirm each stakeholder agrees

personas.name: Sofia
personas.notes: Sofia is usually engaged, but lately her energy in reviews feels lower than normal.
personas.scripted_fallback: not a big thing. running a bit flat, work feels heavier than usual

script.name: Before we get into it, anything you want to make sure we cover today?
script.answer: nothing urgent. maybe just how the current workload's feeling

script.name: How's your energy been over the last couple of weeks?
script.answer: lower than usual. getting through things but with less momentum

script.name: What's been taking the most out of you lately?
script.answer: the redesign has lots of small stakeholder revisions. death by a thousand tweaks

script.name: Is there anything getting in your way that we could clear?
script.answer: feedback comes scattered across three channels → spends a lot of time chasing what's actually wanted

script.name: How are you feeling about the work itself — not the volume, the work?
script.answer: work itself's fine, still likes it. it's the churn around it wearing her down

script.name: What would make the next two weeks feel lighter?
script.answer: wants the feedback consolidated into one round per milestone, not a constant drip

script.name: What's one thing we could change starting this cycle?
script.answer: set one review checkpoint per milestone, hold revisions to that. stop the all-day context-switching

personas.name: Grace
personas.notes: Grace wants to grow as a lead, but she still avoids some harder team decisions.
personas.scripted_fallback: delays the hard calls. doesn't want to be the one who upsets the team's balance

script.name: Before we start, is there anything specific you want to make sure we cover today?
script.answer: wants to talk about growing as a lead. specifically the parts she finds harder

script.name: As a lead, what kind of call do you want to get better at making?
script.answer: the harder team calls — making them earlier, not letting them resolve themselves

script.name: What's a recent hard call you held off on?
script.answer: knew two designers overlapped on the same surface for weeks. didn't reassign until it got tense

script.name: When you delay those calls, what's usually holding you back?
script.answer: holds back to avoid disrupting team harmony → waits for a consensus that never quite comes

script.name: What does the team lose when those decisions wait?
script.answer: team loses momentum and clarity. people keep working without knowing who owns what

script.name: Where could you be steering the team more than you are now?
script.answer: could push harder on how the team makes calls. defaults to facilitating when they need her to decide

script.name: Is there a decision you've been putting off that you could make this week?
script.answer: will set ownership on the shared surface this week, not wait for everyone to agree

personas.name: Nina
personas.notes: Nina's content work is good, but recent handoffs have missed details the team needed.
personas.scripted_fallback: writing's solid. it's the context around it at handoff she's not fully passing on

script.name: Before we start, is there anything specific you want to make sure we cover today?
script.answer: happy to talk handoffs. knows a couple have come back with gaps

script.name: Recent handoffs have missed some details the team needed. Does that line up with what you've seen?
script.answer: agrees. copy's been fine, but eng comes back asking about states she didn't document

script.name: Walk me through a recent handoff that came back with questions.
script.answer: error messaging. wrote the strings but didn't note which maps to which state, so eng had to guess

script.name: What did you know about it that the team didn't get told?
script.answer: knew the logic for when each message shows. obvious to her, so didn't write it down

script.name: What does a complete handoff need to include for the team to run with it?
script.answer: complete handoff = strings, the state each maps to, char limits, fallback for empty/error cases

script.name: What would make that consistent rather than case-by-case?
script.answer: wants a short handoff checklist run every time, not relying on remembering it in the moment

script.name: What's one thing you'll change on the next handoff?
script.answer: will attach a states-to-strings map and the edge cases every time, not just the copy

personas.name: Samira
personas.notes: Samira seems busy all the time, but the work feels scattered and hard to prioritize.
personas.scripted_fallback: busy constantly, but honestly couldn't say which of it actually matters most right now

script.name: Before we get into it, anything you want to make sure we cover today?
script.answer: probably priorities. busy all the time but it feels scattered

script.name: If you had to name your top priority right now, what is it?
script.answer: can't name one. four things all feel equally urgent

script.name: Where's most of your time actually going this week?
script.answer: jumping between the onboarding map, two stakeholder requests, a research readout. none finishing

script.name: If one thing dropped off your plate, what would free up the most?
script.answer: dropping the ad-hoc stakeholder requests would free the most. constant, fragments the bigger work

script.name: Where could I help clear a path or make a call?
script.answer: wants help saying no, or 'later', to the side requests so she can land the onboarding map

script.name: What's making it hard to prioritize on your own?
script.answer: everything comes in as urgent. no clear signal on what's actually most important

script.name: What's one change for the next two weeks?
script.answer: agree her top one or two, route new requests through manager so she can protect that focus

personas.name: Marcus
personas.notes: Marcus is still handling clients, but he seems more drained and less present than usual.
personas.scripted_fallback: client work still gets done. it's everything around it he's got less left for right now

script.name: Before we start, is there anything specific you want to make sure we cover today?
script.answer: nothing on the agenda. happy to just talk through how things are going

script.name: You've seemed a bit more drained lately — does that match how it feels?
script.answer: agrees. keeping up with clients but less in the tank than usual

script.name: How's the client load itself been sitting?
script.answer: heavy. took two of the bigger accounts after the team change, hasn't put them down

script.name: Is any of it weighing on you beyond the hours?
script.answer: couple are tense renewals → carrying that side of it most of the day too

script.name: Outside the clients, is anything internally adding to it?
script.answer: handoffs to support have been rocky → keeps re-absorbing stuff he thought he'd passed on

script.name: What would actually take some of the weight off?
script.answer: wants one of the two big accounts rebalanced, and a cleaner handoff path so things don't bounce back

script.name: What's one thing we put in place this week?
script.answer: move one tense account off him, fix the support handoff so he's not the fallback for everything

personas.name: Aisha
personas.notes: Aisha has pipeline activity, but recent calls are not turning into strong next steps.
personas.scripted_fallback: activity's there. it's the close of the call, locking a real next step, where she keeps losing it

script.name: Before we start, is there anything specific you want to make sure we cover today?
script.answer: wants to look at her calls. lots of activity but it's not converting into next steps

script.name: Think about a recent call that didn't move forward — how did it actually go?
script.answer: good energy, decent rapport. ended vague — 'they'll think about it', no next step booked

script.name: How deep did you get on what's actually driving their decision?
script.answer: not deep enough. covered the product well, didn't pin down their real pain or timeline

script.name: When you close a call, how clear is the next step you're setting?
script.answer: vague usually. says she'll follow up rather than booking the specific next meeting on the spot

script.name: What's the pattern across the calls that stall?
script.answer: talks more than she asks → leaves without a clear read on what they need to say yes

script.name: What would change if discovery were tighter?
script.answer: tighter discovery → she'd know the real blocker, tie the next step to it, not a generic follow-up

script.name: What's one thing you'll do differently on the next call?
script.answer: will lead with discovery, confirm decision criteria, book the specific next meeting before hanging up

personas.name: Ben
personas.notes: Ben is doing the work, but he seems unsure where he is trying to grow next.
personas.scripted_fallback: not sure what he's aiming at yet. just wants it to feel like it's going somewhere

script.name: Before we start, is there anything specific you want to make sure we cover today?
script.answer: wants help figuring out where he's trying to grow. feels a bit stuck on direction

script.name: What kind of work gives you the most energy right now?
script.answer: most energy from fixing a broken process. seeing it run smoother afterward feels good

script.name: And what tends to drain you or feel like a slog?
script.answer: repetitive data entry drains him. fine, but no real challenge or end to it

script.name: Where could you take a bit more ownership of something end to end?
script.answer: could own the vendor onboarding process. touches parts of it but no one runs the whole thing

script.name: What skill would help you take that on?
script.answer: wants more confidence with the reporting side, to show the process is actually improving

script.name: When you picture six months out, what would feel like progress?
script.answer: six months out = owning one process properly, being the go-to for it, not bits of everything

script.name: What's one small step we could set up now?
script.answer: take the lead on vendor onboarding, pair him with someone on reporting to build that up

personas.name: Daniel
personas.notes: Daniel is strong, but recent design decisions have felt slower and more complicated than they needed to be.
personas.scripted_fallback: over-scopes because he's trying to solve the whole problem at once instead of the next decision

script.name: Before we start, is there anything specific you want to make sure we cover today?
script.answer: happy to talk decision speed. knows a few recent calls dragged longer than they should have

script.name: Recent decisions have felt slower and more complex than needed. Does that match your read?
script.answer: agrees. expanding the scope of decisions instead of just making the call in front of him

script.name: Walk me through a recent one that got more complicated than it needed to.
script.answer: settings redesign. small layout call turned into a full rethink of the information architecture

script.name: What made it grow rather than stay contained?
script.answer: kept pulling in adjacent problems because they felt connected, instead of shipping the small fix first

script.name: How are you weighing 'good enough now' against 'complete later'?
script.answer: leans too hard toward complete. wants it fully right, so delays decisions that were fine to make quickly

script.name: How does that pace affect the people working with you?
script.answer: slows the team. they wait on him, start over-scoping too because that's the standard he's modelling

script.name: What's one change you'll make on the next decision?
script.answer: make the smallest call that unblocks the team now, defer the bigger rethink to its own track
